goal: Land the two G7 (Quality and security) external-binary plugin manifests for Phase 3 Week 11 — `plugins/quality/eslint/plugin.toml` (P3-W11-F02) and `plugins/quality/semgrep/plugin.toml` (P3-W11-F04) — plus their devtools install hints, an end-to-end `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs` integration test exercising real `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout` round-trips, and replacements for the TODO-stub acceptance scripts at `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F02.sh` (ESLint JS/TS lint vs. fixture typescript-project) and `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F04.sh` (Semgrep OWASP SAST scan vs. fixture mixed-project) that pair an MCP `tools/list` smoke with a feature-specific tool-level smoke. Direct port of the WO-0075 G6 paired-manifest template (GitHub + Git + Filesystem) scaled-down from 3 to 2 features for the published canonical upstreams: ESLint MCP via `npx @eslint/mcp@0.3.5` (npm; published 2026-05-01) and Semgrep MCP via `uvx semgrep-mcp@0.9.0` (PyPI; published 2025-09-29). Establishes the new `quality.*` capability namespace under `plugins/quality/` (parallel to `context.*`, `knowledge.*`, `architecture.*`, `search.*`, `navigate.*`, `platform.*`). Per WO-0074/0075 lesson §planner: `G5 → G6 → G7/G8 paired-manifest template port — apply same shape to G7..G8`. P3-W11-F03 (Ruff MCP) is EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED from this WO per DEC-0020 — no canonical upstream Ruff MCP server is published to PyPI/npm at this date; F03 is deferred to Phase 4/7 hardening pending upstream emergence.
plan_summary: Mirror the WO-0075 G6 tri-manifest precedent verbatim, retargeted from Platform to Quality group and scaled-down from 3 to 2 features (Ruff deferred per DEC-0020). Two stdio MCP servers — ESLint MCP + Semgrep MCP — get hand-authored TOML manifests under a new `plugins/quality/<name>/plugin.toml` directory tree, each pinned to the verified-published-immutable upstream tag with `transport.type = "stdio"` and a documented `capabilities.provides` list under the `quality.*` namespace. Two devtools install scripts (`scripts/devtools/install-eslint-mcp.sh`, `install-semgrep-mcp.sh`) mirror the `install-github-mcp.sh` / `install-context7-mcp.sh` shape verbatim — informational-only, idempotent, optional warm-up via `--help`. A new `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs` integration test file declares one `mod g7_plugin_manifests { ... }` at file root with two `#[tokio::test] async fn` health-check tests — substring selectors `g7_plugin_manifests::eslint_manifest_health_check` and `g7_plugin_manifests::semgrep_manifest_health_check` MUST resolve cleanly per DEC-0007 + WO-0069/0074/0075 lessons §"For planner: module-root placement". Two `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F0{2,4}.sh` scripts replace the TODO stubs and run the targeted `cargo test` selector plus a tool-level smoke (gated by `UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E` per the WO-0074/0075 `UCIL_SKIP_<GROUP>_PLUGIN_E2E` precedent, defaulting to RUN). The M1/M2 mutation contract is pre-baked verbatim from WO-0074/0075 §10/§11: M1 poisons `transport.command` per manifest (2× M1), M2 replaces the expected canonical tool name in the test file (2× M2 — executor MUST confirm exact tool names from live `tools/list` BEFORE choosing the literal, per WO-0074/0075 lesson §executor #1: `DON'T translate live tools/list names to snake_case when they ship kebab-case` — both ESLint and Semgrep MCP tool names are TBD-by-live-capture since neither upstream README enumerates them in this snapshot). No daemon source changes; no `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` edits permitted. F02 ESLint smoke runs against a tmpdir copy of `tests/fixtures/typescript-project` (which has package.json + tsconfig.json + src/ but may NOT carry an eslint config — executor MUST fabricate a minimal `eslint.config.js` in the tmpdir per fixture-mutation invariant; OR use ESLint defaults if the upstream MCP server supports zero-config inspection of TS files). F04 Semgrep smoke runs against a tmpdir copy of `tests/fixtures/mixed-project` and asserts ≥1 OWASP-class finding via the upstream's default OWASP rule set (Semgrep ships with a free OWASP Top-10 ruleset by default — no auth required). Per WO-0075 §executor lesson W1: ALL `tokio::test` async helpers MUST use `tokio::process::Command` (NOT `std::process::Command`) for fixture-init / git-init / tmpdir-mutation operations — pre-emptive carve-out to avoid the WO-0075 W1 critic warning.
scope_in: 1. NEW plugin manifest at `plugins/quality/eslint/plugin.toml` for the official ESLint MCP server published to npm at `@eslint/mcp@0.3.5` (latest publish 2026-05-01 confirmed via npm registry pre-flight). Required keys: `[plugin] name="eslint" category="quality" priority="P0" version="0.3.5"`; `[transport] type="stdio" command="npx" args=["-y", "@eslint/mcp@0.3.5"]`; `[capabilities] provides=["quality.eslint.lint", "quality.eslint.fix"]` (executor: confirm exact tool names by spawning the live binary and capturing the canonical `tools/list` response BEFORE writing the literal — prefer kebab-case-as-emitted-by-upstream over snake-case translation per WO-0074/0075 lesson §executor #1; the master-plan §4.7 vocabulary `lint` etc. is the capability NAME independent of the upstream's literal tool name; the npm bin entry is `mcp` per `npm view @eslint/mcp bin` per pre-flight); `[lifecycle] hot_cold=true idle_timeout_minutes=10` mirrors the ast-grep / Context7 / GitHub-MCP pattern. `[capabilities] activation.on_language=["javascript", "typescript"]` and `activation.on_tool=["check_quality", "review_changes"]` per master-plan §14.1 line 1455+ shape. Top-of-file rustdoc comment must document: source URL (https://github.com/eslint/rewrite/tree/main/packages/mcp), npm package name (`@eslint/mcp`), pinned tag (`0.3.5`), binary launch command, advertised tools (full live list from the live `tools/list` capture per scope_in #14), transport type (stdio), API key requirements (NONE — runs entirely locally; no auth), and the working-directory invariant (the upstream binary inspects the cwd or the explicitly-passed file paths — the integration test + verify script MUST copy `tests/fixtures/typescript-project` into a `mktemp -d` tmpdir, optionally fabricate a minimal `eslint.config.js` in the tmpdir if upstream requires one, and invoke from the tmpdir).

2. NEW plugin manifest at `plugins/quality/semgrep/plugin.toml` for the official Semgrep MCP server published to PyPI at `semgrep-mcp@0.9.0` (latest publish 2025-09-29 confirmed via PyPI pre-flight). Required keys: `[plugin] name="semgrep" category="quality" priority="P0" version="0.9.0"`; `[transport] type="stdio" command="uvx" args=["semgrep-mcp@0.9.0"]` (note: master-plan §14.1 line 1464 uses the legacy `command = "semgrep" args = ["--mcp"]` form, but the canonical Astral/Semgrep upstream is now the dedicated `semgrep-mcp` package on PyPI — executor MUST use the dedicated server and document the deviation in the manifest top-of-file rustdoc + the WO RFR §"Disclosed Deviations"); `[capabilities] provides=["quality.semgrep.scan", "quality.semgrep.security_rules", "quality.semgrep.code_quality"]` (executor: confirm exact tool names from live `tools/list`); `[capabilities] languages=["python", "typescript", "rust", "go", "java", "c", "cpp"]` per master-plan §14.1 line 1457; `[capabilities] activation.on_tool=["check_quality", "security_scan", "review_changes"]`; `[lifecycle]` mirrors. Top-of-file rustdoc must document: source URL (https://github.com/semgrep/mcp), PyPI package name (`semgrep-mcp`), pinned tag (`0.9.0`), binary launch command, advertised tools (full live list), transport type (stdio default per upstream README), API key requirements (NONE for basic SAST scan with default OWASP ruleset; an OPTIONAL `SEMGREP_APP_TOKEN` env var enables Semgrep AppSec Platform integration but is NOT required for the verify script's basic-scan smoke per upstream README), and the OWASP-default-ruleset claim (executor MUST verify by running a live `semgrep_scan` `tools/call` against `tests/fixtures/mixed-project` and confirming ≥1 OWASP-class finding emerges with the bare default ruleset; the F04 spec line `SAST scan of the fixture mixed-project detects ≥1 security finding using the OWASP rule set` is the load-bearing claim).

3. NEW devtools script at `scripts/devtools/install-eslint-mcp.sh`. Idempotent bash; verifies node + npm available; runs `npx -y @eslint/mcp@0.3.5 --help 2>&1 | head -1` (or analogous warm-up that triggers npm fetch + cache without blocking on stdin) to ensure the package is in the npm cache for the integration test's first spawn. Must `set -euo pipefail`; must exit 0 on success; must NOT require sudo; must NOT actually install anything (informational scaffolding only — mirrors `install-github-mcp.sh` shape verbatim per WO-0075). Document the working-directory invariant (the test/verify script handles cwd, NOT the install script) as a `[NOTE]` block at the bottom (informational only — operator-state, NOT actionable by the script).

4. NEW devtools script at `scripts/devtools/install-semgrep-mcp.sh`. Idempotent bash; verifies python3 + uv (or uvx) available (NOT node — Semgrep MCP is pypi-distributed); runs `uvx semgrep-mcp@0.9.0 --help 2>&1 | head -1` for the warm-up. Mirrors `install-mem0-mcp.sh` / `install-git-mcp.sh` shape but adapted for Semgrep. Must NOT install uv if missing (operator-state); EMIT a `[MISSING]` notice if uv/uvx is not on PATH and exit 0 gracefully. Must NOT require sudo. Top-of-file documents the optional `SEMGREP_APP_TOKEN` env var (informational only — the F04 verify script does NOT require auth for the basic-scan smoke).

5. NEW integration-test file at `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs`. Top of file: mirror existing `g6_plugin_manifests.rs` shape verbatim — same imports, same helper-function placement, same tokio runtime flavor declaration, same `//!` rustdoc declaring mocking is FORBIDDEN. CRITICAL DEVIATION FROM WO-0075 W1 LESSON: ALL fixture-init / cwd-mutation / tmpdir-population helpers in async test bodies MUST use `tokio::process::Command` (NOT `std::process::Command`), per WO-0075 lesson §executor: `In async integration tests, use tokio::process::Command even for fixture-setup helpers... .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Async line 23 mandates tokio::process::Command in async paths`. Single `mod g7_plugin_manifests { ... }` block at file root containing TWO `#[tokio::test] async fn` tests — `eslint_manifest_health_check`, `semgrep_manifest_health_check`. Each test: (a) loads the corresponding `plugins/quality/<name>/plugin.toml` via the daemon's existing manifest loader (use the same APIs as g3/g4/g5/g6_plugin_manifests.rs); (b) for ESLint: copy `tests/fixtures/typescript-project` into a `mktemp -d` tmpdir using `tokio::process::Command` (or `tokio::fs::copy` + `walkdir` / `async_walkdir`), optionally fabricate a minimal `eslint.config.js` in the tmpdir if the upstream binary requires one (fabricate using `tokio::fs::write`); for Semgrep: copy `tests/fixtures/mixed-project` into a `mktemp -d` tmpdir similarly; (c) constructs a `PluginManager` (or analogous abstraction) and calls `health_check_with_timeout` (or the equivalent method that round-trips a real MCP `tools/list` over stdio); (d) asserts the live response carries a specific canonical tool name (executor confirms via live capture; e.g. `lint` for ESLint OR whatever `@eslint/mcp@0.3.5` advertises; `semgrep_scan` for Semgrep OR whatever `semgrep-mcp@0.9.0` advertises); (e) panics with `(SAn) <semantic_name>; got: <full-live-tool-list>` on assertion failure per DEC-0007 SA-numbered panic-body format. Substring selectors `g7_plugin_manifests::eslint_manifest_health_check`, `g7_plugin_manifests::semgrep_manifest_health_check` MUST resolve cleanly via `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g7_plugin_manifests <selector>`. NO `mod tests { ... }` nesting; the test functions live at `mod g7_plugin_manifests` ROOT (NOT inside a nested `mod tests` per DEC-0007 + WO-0069/0074/0075 lessons §"For planner"). Mirror the existing `g6_plugin_manifests.rs` file shape verbatim. Provide a `skip_via_env()` helper checking `UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E` per WO-0074/0075 lesson §executor #3 (document inline; default to RUN; verifier MUST NOT set the opt-out per scope_in #15).

6. NEW `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F02.sh` (replace TODO stub) for ESLint MCP. `set -euo pipefail`. (a) Sanity-check `plugins/quality/eslint/plugin.toml` parses as TOML and asserts the keys named in scope_in #1 via `python3 -c 'import tomllib; ...'`. (b) Run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g7_plugin_manifests g7_plugin_manifests::eslint_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | tee /tmp/wo-0076-f02-cargo.log` and `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed' /tmp/wo-0076-f02-cargo.log`. (c) Tool-level smoke (per WO-0074/0075 lesson §executor #4: prefer python polling with deadline over bash sleep): copy `tests/fixtures/typescript-project` into `mktemp -d` tmpdir BEFORE invoking the upstream binary (per WO-0074/0075 lesson §executor #5); optionally fabricate a minimal `eslint.config.js` in the tmpdir if upstream requires it (executor confirms during live capture; the file SHOULD use `import js from "@eslint/js"; export default [js.configs.recommended];` or analogous minimal configuration that produces ≥1 lint finding against the fixture's TS source — adjust if the upstream's MCP `lint` tool ships a default cfg); spawn the ESLint MCP server via canonical `npx -y @eslint/mcp@0.3.5` from the tmpdir as cwd; send an MCP `initialize` + `notifications/initialized` + `tools/list` JSON-RPC sequence over stdio (use python with 60-second deadline polling per WO-0074 §executor #4); assert `tools/list` response carries the upstream's canonical lint tool name; send a `tools/call` for the lint tool with the fixture's TS source paths; assert response contains structured lint findings with at least file/line/rule/severity fields per F02 spec line `JS/TS lint errors from the fixture typescript-project returned with file, line, rule, and severity`. Gate the entire tool-level smoke section behind `UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E` env-var per the WO-0074/0075 precedent — DEFAULT to RUN. Final exit 0 on success; non-zero on any check failure with a clear `>&2` diagnostic. The cargo-test `tools/list` path is the load-bearing assertion; the tool-level lint smoke is the additional smoke per F02 spec.

7. NEW `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F04.sh` (replace TODO stub) for Semgrep MCP. `set -euo pipefail`. (a) Sanity-check `plugins/quality/semgrep/plugin.toml` parses as TOML and asserts keys per scope_in #2. (b) Cargo-test selector for `g7_plugin_manifests::semgrep_manifest_health_check`. (c) Tool-level smoke: copy `tests/fixtures/mixed-project` into `mktemp -d` tmpdir BEFORE invoking the upstream binary; spawn `uvx semgrep-mcp@0.9.0` (with stdio transport — Semgrep MCP defaults to stdio per upstream README) from a working directory that lets it reach the tmpdir; send `initialize` + `notifications/initialized` + `tools/list` + `tools/call` JSON-RPC sequence — `tools/call` invokes the upstream's canonical scan tool (executor confirms exact name from live `tools/list`; e.g. `semgrep_scan` or analogous) with a `path` argument pointing at the tmpdir AND a `config` argument selecting the OWASP ruleset (executor MUST confirm the canonical OWASP ruleset name from upstream — Semgrep ships with `p/owasp-top-ten` as a registry-named ruleset; alternative is `p/security-audit` which includes OWASP-class rules; the F04 spec line is `≥1 security finding using the OWASP rule set` — executor picks whichever Semgrep registry name maps to OWASP and documents the choice). Use python with 60-second deadline polling. Assert the response is a successful JSON-RPC reply and the findings array contains ≥1 finding with severity / rule_id / file / line fields per F04 spec line `≥1 security finding using the OWASP rule set`. Gate behind `UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E`; DEFAULT to RUN. Final exit 0/non-zero with diagnostics on stderr. Optional second smoke: `tools/call` with a different OWASP-class rule and assert ≥1 finding (informational, NOT load-bearing).

8. M1 mutation contract (transport.command poison; runs once per feature, two M1 mutations total). Pre-mutation per feature: `md5sum plugins/quality/<name>/plugin.toml > /tmp/wo-0076-<name>-orig.md5` (capturing snapshot BEFORE any edit). Mutation: edit the targeted `plugin.toml` and replace `command = "npx"` (ESLint) or `command = "uvx"` (Semgrep) with `command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"`. Selector: the targeted health-check test under `g7_plugin_manifests`. Expected panic: a `PluginError::Spawn { source: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound } }` chain (or analogous `tokio::process::Command::spawn` ENOENT-class error). Restore: `git checkout -- plugins/quality/<name>/plugin.toml`. Confirm: `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0076-<name>-orig.md5` returns OK. Mirror the WO-0074/0075 §10 verbatim shape for both manifests in turn.

9. M2 mutation contract (expected-tool-name regression; runs once per feature, two M2 mutations total). Pre-mutation: `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs > /tmp/wo-0076-g7tests-orig.md5` (one snapshot covers both since the file is shared across tests; verifier may apply mutations sequentially, restoring between each). Mutation: edit the test file and replace whichever string the targeted test asserts as the canonical tool name (executor will have selected each from the live `tools/list` response per scope_in #14) with `"NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M2"`. Selector: the targeted test. Expected: structured panic carrying the full live tool-list (`(SAn) <semantic>; got: ["<live tool 1>", "<live tool 2>", ...]; want: "NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M2"`). Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs`. Confirm via md5. Mirror the WO-0074/0075 §11 verbatim shape.

10. Commit cadence: aim for 7-9 atomic commits per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md` ~50 LOC soft target (2× plugin.toml manifests at 60-90 LOC each = candidates for DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out citation; 2× install scripts at ~70 LOC; 1× integration test file at 200-300 LOC under DEC-0005 carve-out cited in commit body; 2× verify scripts at 200-400 LOC each — F04 may be larger due to tmpdir + python polling + finding-shape assertions; 1× RFR). Suggested order: (i) plugins/quality/eslint/plugin.toml + install-eslint-mcp.sh (paired commit OR two adjacent commits); (ii) plugins/quality/semgrep/plugin.toml + install-semgrep-mcp.sh; (iii) crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs (one cohesive feat commit covering both #[tokio::test] async fns under DEC-0005 carve-out — cite in commit body; pre-emptively use `tokio::process::Command` for any fixture-init helpers per WO-0075 W1 lesson); (iv) scripts/verify/P3-W11-F02.sh; (v) scripts/verify/P3-W11-F04.sh; (vi) RFR. Each commit Conventional-Commit-formatted with `Phase: 3` + correct `Feature:` (or both for shared-infra commits like the integration test file) + `Work-order: WO-0076` trailers.

11. Standing AC23 substantive coverage protocol per WO-0067..WO-0075 carryover (now 32 WOs deep). Use `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` as the authoritative measurement (per WO-0074/0075 lesson §executor: the literal `--no-default-features --features default` arg-pair errors because ucil-daemon has no `default` feature defined). WO is purely additive on tests + manifests + scripts — pre-WO baseline must be preserved (no `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` source changes per scope_out #2). Document the measurement in the RFR.

12. Apply WO-0075 lessons §"For planner" + §"For executor" + §"For verifier" verbatim: tests at module root (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`); DEC-0007 SA-numbered panic-body format on every assert; DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam pattern for any future production-wiring follow-up (NOT in this WO scope — this WO is manifests-only); cite WO-0044 + WO-0069 + WO-0072 + WO-0074 + WO-0075 lessons by ID in the RFR's §"Lessons applied" section. Specific WO-0075 lessons to apply verbatim: (a) §executor W1: `In async integration tests, use tokio::process::Command even for fixture-setup helpers` — pre-emptively use the async form for ALL fixture-init / tmpdir-mutation operations in #[tokio::test] async fn bodies (avoids the WO-0075 critic warning); (b) §executor #1: `DON'T translate live tools/list names to snake_case when they ship kebab-case` — capture canonical names from live binary BEFORE writing assertion literals (live capture protocol per scope_in #14); (c) §executor #2: avoid invoking the binary in MCP-server mode during warm-up (use `--help`); (d) §executor #3: document `UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E` in test rustdoc + both verify scripts; (e) §executor #4: prefer python polling with deadline over bash sleep for tools/call wall-time-sensitive smokes (Semgrep scans may run several seconds; python with 60-second deadline is canonical); (f) §executor #5: `DO copy tests/fixtures/<project> into a mktemp -d tmpdir BEFORE invoking` — required for both F02 (typescript-project) and F04 (mixed-project); (g) §verifier: `Ensure UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS and UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E are NOT exported when running cargo test g7_plugin_manifests::* and bash scripts/verify/P3-W11-F0{2,4}.sh`.

13. Pre-flight commands the executor MUST run before first push: (a) `cargo build -p ucil-daemon` — clean compile; (b) `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g7_plugin_manifests` — both frozen tests green; (c) `cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets --tests -- -D warnings` — zero warnings; (d) `cargo fmt -p ucil-daemon --check` — formatting clean; (e) word-ban grep on production-side files: `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/quality/{eslint,semgrep}/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-{eslint,semgrep}-mcp.sh scripts/verify/P3-W11-F0{2,4}.sh` returns empty (the `//! Mocking ... is FORBIDDEN` rustdoc declaration in the test file is exempt under WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0072/0075 §executor `Negative assertion forbidding mocking, NOT a mock implementation`); (f) `bash scripts/verify/P3-W11-F02.sh` + `bash scripts/verify/P3-W11-F04.sh` both green; (g) `command -v npx uvx python3` confirms PATH has all three required runtimes — the verifier requires the same.

14. Live-tool-name capture protocol (WO-0074/0075 §executor #1 lesson — CRITICAL for M2 mutation correctness): BEFORE writing any tool-name assertion literal, the executor MUST: (a) for ESLint MCP — run the canonical npx command with stdin frames driving past `initialize` → `notifications/initialized` → `tools/list`; capture the `tools/list` response's `result.tools[].name` array verbatim; embed this list in the manifest's top-of-file rustdoc comment + the integration test's panic-body string. (b) Repeat for Semgrep MCP via `uvx semgrep-mcp@0.9.0`. Choose ONE canonical tool name per manifest as the M2 assertion target — prefer the most-stable / most-documented one (e.g., ESLint's primary lint tool over edge-case fix-all tool; Semgrep's primary `semgrep_scan` or analogous over edge-case rule-search). Document the chosen literals + the full live tool-list in the RFR's `## Live tool name capture` section per the WO-0074/0075 RFR template. CRITICAL: BOTH upstream README (eslint.org/docs/latest/use/mcp + github.com/semgrep/mcp) DO NOT enumerate the advertised tools — the executor's live capture is the ONLY source of truth.

15. Capability namespace establishment (`quality.*`): parallel to `search.*` (probe), `navigate.*` (scip), `knowledge.*` (codebase-memory + mem0), `architecture.*` (codegraphcontext), `context.*` (Context7 + Repomix), `platform.*` (GitHub + Git + Filesystem). G7 Quality sources advertise capabilities under the `quality.*` prefix; subdivisions per source: `quality.eslint.*`, `quality.semgrep.*`. Subsequent G7 plugins (Ruff per DEC-0020 deferral; Sonarqube / Snyk / OSV / RuboCop / Trivy / TruffleHog / Gitleaks per master-plan §4.7 line 378-384) MUST extend this namespace consistently when they land in future WOs.

16. ESLint config-file fabrication for F02 smoke. The fixture `tests/fixtures/typescript-project` carries `package.json` + `tsconfig.json` + `src/` but does NOT carry an ESLint config file (per pre-flight `ls`). The ESLint MCP server (`@eslint/mcp@0.3.5`) MAY require an ESLint config in the cwd to produce lint findings. Executor MUST: (a) during live capture, determine whether `@eslint/mcp@0.3.5` runs zero-config (defaulting to ESLint's built-in recommended rules) OR requires an explicit `eslint.config.js` / `.eslintrc.json`; (b) if config is required, the integration test + verify script MUST fabricate a minimal `eslint.config.js` in the tmpdir (NOT in the fixture — fixture is read-only per `forbidden_paths`). Suggested minimal content: `import js from "@eslint/js"; export default [js.configs.recommended, { files: ["**/*.{js,mjs,cjs,ts,tsx}"] }];` or analogous; (c) document the chosen approach in the manifest top-of-file rustdoc + the test file's `//!` rustdoc; (d) the F02 spec line `JS/TS lint errors from the fixture typescript-project returned with file, line, rule, and severity` REQUIRES the smoke to actually surface lint findings — if zero-config + the fixture's TS source produces zero findings, the executor MUST either inject a deliberate-lint-violation file in the tmpdir (e.g., `bad.ts` containing `var x = 1; // var is forbidden by recommended`) OR use a strictER config. Document the choice in the RFR.

17. Forbidden-path audits the executor MUST run BEFORE pushing. Every entry in `forbidden_paths` (scope-out #16) MUST produce `git diff main feat -- <path>` returning empty. Specifically: (a) `git diff main feat -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/` — empty (no daemon source changes; this WO is manifests-only); (b) `git diff main feat -- crates/ucil-core/src/` — empty; (c) `git diff main feat -- tests/fixtures/` — empty (fixtures are read-only; tmpdir copies in tests/scripts are NOT fixtures); (d) `git diff main feat -- ucil-build/feature-list.json ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md` — empty (oracle docs are immutable); (e) `git diff main feat -- ucil-build/decisions/` — empty (DEC-0020 was pre-authored by the planner alongside this WO; ADRs are planner-territory); (f) `git diff main feat -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g{3,4,5,6}_plugin_manifests.rs crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` — empty (prior group tests are isolated per WO-0069 §executor #2 separation invariant); (g) `git diff main feat -- plugins/{architecture,context,knowledge,platform,search,structural,testing}/` — empty (other groups' plugins untouched). Cite WO-0072 scope_in #2 (six-audit checklist) and WO-0075 §executor (post-cherry-pick / post-edit audit shape) as the audit template.

18. scope_out: 1. NO P3-W11-F03 (Ruff MCP plugin) — DEFERRED per DEC-0020 to Phase 4/7 hardening pending upstream availability (no canonical PyPI/npm Ruff MCP server published at this date; pre-flight sweep documented in DEC-0020 §Context). The next planner pass with reason to believe upstream has shipped MUST re-check via the DEC-0020 §Revisit-trigger curl/npm/gh script and emit a new WO if applicable.

2. NO `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` source changes. WO is purely additive on `plugins/quality/`, `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/`, `scripts/devtools/`, `scripts/verify/`. Critic precedent at WO-0071 (DEC-0019) flags forbidden_paths violations on daemon-src changes for plugin-manifest-scoped WOs.

3. NO G7 group runtime in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`. That's the WO-0073/0070-equivalent G7 follow-up (G3→G4→G5→G6→G7 template port per WO-0073/0074/0075 lessons §"For planner: G-group recipe") and lands as a separate WO consuming the ESLint + Semgrep + (eventually) Ruff plugin manifests from this WO + its successor. F05 (G7 severity-weighted merge fusion) is the dep-blocked feature ID for that follow-up — currently dep-blocked on F01 not F02/F04 alone, so this WO does NOT advance F05's readiness directly but sets up the manifests.
4. NO classify_query / parse_reason daemon-side wiring (deferred since WO-0067/0068). Continues deferred orthogonal to this WO's plugin-manifest scope.

5. NO new ADR creation under `ucil-build/decisions/**` BY THE EXECUTOR. The DEC-0020 ADR is pre-authored by the planner alongside this WO; no further ADRs are required for this WO's scope. ADRs are planner-territory (DEC-0019 critic precedent at WO-0071 attempt-2). If the executor encounters an ambiguity that requires an ADR, escalate via `/escalate` rather than authoring inline.

6. NO modifications to `tests/fixtures/**`. The fixtures `typescript-project` + `mixed-project` are read-only assets — F02 verify script + ESLint integration test copy `typescript-project` into a tmpdir before potentially adding an `eslint.config.js`; F04 verify script + Semgrep integration test copy `mixed-project` into a tmpdir before invoking the upstream binary. NO edits to the fixture sources themselves.
7. NO new entries in `feature-list.json`. F02 + F04 entries already exist with frozen `id` + `description` + `acceptance_tests` + `dependencies` per the no-mid-phase-additions rule.

8. NO P3-W9-F11 (incremental computation integration test) work. Per WO-0073 lessons §"For planner: F11 is orthogonal to G4" — F11 is Salsa-engine + tree-sitter pipeline surface, NOT a quality group manifest. Separate WO.
9. NO follow-up daemon spurious-frame-drain code change (DEC-0019 §3 Phase 7 hardening). Unrelated to G7 plugins.
10. NO P3-W9-F10 graphiti plugin manifest. Deferred to Phase 7 per DEC-0019 — not part of this WO.

11. NO G7 fusion (F05) — depends on F01 (LSP diagnostics bridge wired into G7) per the F05 dependency declaration. Lands in a separate G7 production-wiring WO that consumes the manifests from this WO.

12. NO Sonarqube / Snyk / OSV / RuboCop / Trivy / TruffleHog / Gitleaks / Biome / DeepSource / Codacy plugins. Each is a separate feature with its own future WO (master-plan §4.7 line 378-388). This WO ships F02 (ESLint) + F04 (Semgrep) — the two G7 plugins with stable canonical upstream pins as of 2026-05-08.

13. NO write-side filesystem operations against the fixtures. The integration test + verify scripts copy fixtures into tmpdirs and operate against the tmpdir copies — never the fixture sources themselves (forbidden_paths invariant).

14. NO production-wiring of `EsLintG7Source` / `SemgrepG7Source` traits or impls. Those are part of the G7 production-wiring follow-up WO (analogous to G1/G3/G4/G5/G6 production-wiring deferrals per WO-0070/0073/0074/0075 lessons).

15. Effectiveness-gate.sh claude-p sub-session timeout flake (3 open escalations: `20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md`, `20260507T1629Z-effectiveness-refactor-rename-python-fixture-missing-symbol.md`, `20260507T1930Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-doctest-caller-flake.md`) — pre-existing standing scope_out, awaiting dedicated harness-improvement WO. WO-0067..WO-0075 carryover, now 9 WOs deep.

16. `scripts/reality-check.sh` pre-existing-stash bug (carries from WO-0067..WO-0075). Workaround unchanged: M1/M2 in-place mutation contract is the authoritative anti-laziness layer; reality-check.sh is informational only.

17. `scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh` harness improvement (now 32 WOs deep; intermittent — sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER interaction). Continue substantive AC23 coverage check via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov` workaround. Bucket B / Bucket D triage candidate. WO-0074/0075 reported the sccache interaction surfaced again; WO-0072 reported clean — intermittent.
acceptance: Two stdio MCP plugin manifests exist under `plugins/quality/` (eslint, semgrep) declaring `quality.*` capability namespace and pinning npm/pypi-package versions for upstream `@eslint/mcp@0.3.5` and `semgrep-mcp@0.9.0`.
Two devtools install scripts exist under `scripts/devtools/install-{eslint,semgrep}-mcp.sh` and warm npm/uvx cache for the pinned package versions.

One integration-test file `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs` declares `mod g7_plugin_manifests { ... }` at file root with two `#[tokio::test] async fn` tests; substring selectors `g7_plugin_manifests::eslint_manifest_health_check`, `g7_plugin_manifests::semgrep_manifest_health_check` resolve cleanly via `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g7_plugin_manifests <selector>`.
Both verify scripts (P3-W11-F02 + P3-W11-F04) replace the TODO stubs with real implementations exiting 0 on a green run.

Both M1 (transport.command poison) and M2 (expected-tool-name regression) mutation contracts produce SA-tagged panics with the WO-0074/0075 §10/§11 shape; restore-and-md5-confirm protocol verifies independence — two M1 + two M2 mutations applied per feature.

F02 ESLint MCP smoke runs against a tmpdir copy of `tests/fixtures/typescript-project` (with optional fabricated `eslint.config.js` if upstream requires one) and produces structured lint findings with file/line/rule/severity per F02 spec.

F04 Semgrep MCP smoke runs against a tmpdir copy of `tests/fixtures/mixed-project` and produces ≥1 finding with severity / rule_id / file / line fields using the OWASP-class default ruleset per F04 spec.
All `tokio::test` async fn helpers use `tokio::process::Command` (NOT `std::process::Command`) for fixture-init / tmpdir-mutation operations per WO-0075 W1 lesson — pre-emptive carve-out.
No `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` source changes. No `tests/fixtures/**` modifications. No new feature-list.json entries. No new ADRs (DEC-0020 is pre-authored by the planner alongside this WO).
All commits Conventional-Commit-formatted with `Phase: 3` + correct `Feature:` + `Work-order: WO-0076` trailers.
ucil-daemon line coverage preserved at >= pre-WO baseline (>= 85% floor per master plan §15.4) measured via the `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov` workaround per WO-0074/0075 standing protocol.
Live tool-name capture documented in the RFR (Section §"Live tool name capture") with the canonical `tools/list` response from each upstream binary at the chosen pinned version.
RFR §"Lessons applied" section cites DEC-0020 Ruff deferral lineage and the upstream-availability sweep (per scope_in #18).
acceptance_criteria: python3 -c 'import tomllib; m=tomllib.load(open("plugins/quality/eslint/plugin.toml","rb")); assert m["plugin"]["name"]=="eslint"; assert m["plugin"]["category"]=="quality"; assert m["transport"]["type"]=="stdio"; assert any(c.startswith("quality.") for c in m["capabilities"]["provides"])'

python3 -c 'import tomllib; m=tomllib.load(open("plugins/quality/semgrep/plugin.toml","rb")); assert m["plugin"]["name"]=="semgrep"; assert m["plugin"]["category"]=="quality"; assert m["transport"]["type"]=="stdio"; assert any(c.startswith("quality.") for c in m["capabilities"]["provides"])'
cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g7_plugin_manifests g7_plugin_manifests::eslint_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed'
cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g7_plugin_manifests g7_plugin_manifests::semgrep_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed'

! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/quality/eslint/plugin.toml plugins/quality/semgrep/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-eslint-mcp.sh scripts/devtools/install-semgrep-mcp.sh scripts/verify/P3-W11-F02.sh scripts/verify/P3-W11-F04.sh

git diff main..feat/WO-0076-eslint-and-semgrep-quality-plugin-manifests -- crates/ucil-core/src/ | wc -l | xargs test 0 -eq
git diff main..feat/WO-0076-eslint-and-semgrep-quality-plugin-manifests -- tests/fixtures/ | wc -l | xargs test 0 -eq
git diff main..feat/WO-0076-eslint-and-semgrep-quality-plugin-manifests -- ucil-build/feature-list.json ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md | wc -l | xargs test 0 -eq
